claimText: Four vulture species in India, namely the White-rumped, Indian, Slender-billed, and Red-headed vultures, are classified as Critically Endangered (CR).
evidenceExcerpt: Four vulture species in India are Critically Endangered (CR): White-rumped, Indian, Slender-billed, and Red-headed vultures.
examUse: Statement-based question on the IUCN status of specific vulture species in India.
distractorHooks: Classifying these four species as Endangered (EN) or Vulnerable (VU)
Including the Egyptian Vulture in the Critically Endangered list

claimText: The Egyptian Vulture is classified as Endangered (EN), while the Bearded, Cinereous, and Himalayan Griffon vultures are Near Threatened (NT).
evidenceExcerpt: The Egyptian Vulture is Endangered (EN), while Bearded, Cinereous, and Himalayan Griffon are Near Threatened (NT).
examUse: Correct-pair question matching vulture species to their respective IUCN Red List status.
distractorHooks: Classifying the Egyptian Vulture as Critically Endangered (CR)
Classifying the Himalayan Griffon as Endangered (EN)

claimText: The ingestion of toxic non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) like diclofenac, which are used to treat livestock, is a major threat to vulture populations.
evidenceExcerpt: A major threat to vultures is the ingestion of toxic non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) like diclofenac used to treat livestock.
examUse: Statement-based question on the primary causes of the decline in vulture populations in India.
distractorHooks: Stating that antibiotics or vaccines are the primary pharmaceutical threat
Claiming the decline is primarily due to poaching for feathers

claimText: Vulture Conservation Breeding Centres (VCBC) are located at Rani in Assam and Pinjore in Haryana.
evidenceExcerpt: Vulture Conservation Breeding Centres (VCBC) are located at Rani (Assam) and Pinjore (Haryana).
examUse: Location-based correct-pair question matching conservation centers to their respective states.
distractorHooks: Placing Rani in Haryana and Pinjore in Punjab
Stating that VCBCs are exclusively located in South India

claimText: According to the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) assessment, 54% of all documented vulture nests are located within Protected Areas.
evidenceExcerpt: Protected Areas hold 54% of all documented vulture nests according to the WII assessment.
examUse: Statement-based question regarding the nesting habitats and distribution of vultures in India.
distractorHooks: Claiming that over 90% of vulture nests are found outside Protected Areas
Stating that vultures do not nest in Protected Areas due to human interference
